cruise-control.notes: Scores 100 when model produces valid JSON tool calls. 0 scores caused by qwen3-30b intermittently emitting malformed JSON arguments (trailing newlines in JSON), not by incorrect logic.

temperature-control.notes: Original skill failed because model tried to manually compute 500 timesteps instead of running Python. Optimized skill explicitly instructs write-script-then-execute workflow. 0 scores caused by malformed JSON, 90 caused by missing errors array length check.

changes_made: Restructured skill from reference documentation to step-by-step workflow instructions
Added explicit 'write Python script then execute with python3' workflow at top
Simplified PID controller class (removed optional integral_limit parameter for clarity)
Added specific PID gains recommendation for cruise control (Kp=2.0, Ki=0.1, Kd=0.5)
Emphasized output array length requirements (MUST have exactly total_steps elements)
Removed verbose 'When to Use' section and redundant code examples to reduce context size
Consolidated thermal and cruise control guidance into concise code snippets
Added integral_limit guidance for cruise control gap maintenance
key_finding: qwen3-30b intermittently produces malformed JSON for tool call arguments (e.g., trailing newlines inside JSON strings), causing ~50% of runs to fail at the tool-call level regardless of skill content. When valid JSON is produced, both tasks score 100/100 with the optimized skill.
